root_text.an5.159:1.2: ekaṁ samayaṁ bhagavā kosambiyaṁ viharati ghositārāme.
root_text.an5.159:1.3: Tena kho pana samayena āyasmā udāyī mahatiyā gihiparisāya parivuto dhammaṁ desento nisinno hoti.
root_text.an5.159:1.4: Addasā kho āyasmā ānando āyasmantaṁ udāyiṁ mahatiyā gihiparisāya parivutaṁ dhammaṁ desentaṁ nisinnaṁ.
root_text.an5.159:1.5: Disvā yena bhagavā tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā bhagavantaṁ abhivādetvā ekamantaṁ nisīdi. Ekamantaṁ nisinno kho āyasmā ānando bhagavantaṁ etadavoca:
root_text.an5.159:1.6: “āyasmā, bhante, udāyī mahatiyā gihiparisāya parivuto dhammaṁ desetī”ti.
root_text.an5.159:2.1: “Na kho, ānanda, sukaraṁ paresaṁ dhammaṁ desetuṁ.
root_text.an5.159:2.2: Paresaṁ, ānanda, dhammaṁ desentena pañca dhamme ajjhattaṁ upaṭṭhāpetvā paresaṁ dhammo desetabbo.
root_text.an5.159:2.6: ‘anuddayataṁ paṭicca kathaṁ kathessāmī’ti paresaṁ dhammo desetabbo;
root_text.an5.159:2.7: ‘na āmisantaro kathaṁ kathessāmī’ti paresaṁ dhammo desetabbo;
root_text.an5.159:2.8: ‘attānañca parañca anupahacca kathaṁ kathessāmī’ti paresaṁ dhammo desetabbo.
root_text.an5.159:2.9: Na kho, ānanda, sukaraṁ paresaṁ dhammaṁ desetuṁ.
root_text.an5.159:2.10: Paresaṁ, ānanda, dhammaṁ desentena ime pañca dhamme ajjhattaṁ upaṭṭhāpetvā paresaṁ dhammo desetabbo”ti.

translation_text.an5.159:1.2: At one time the Buddha was staying near Kosambī, in Ghosita’s Monastery.
translation_text.an5.159:1.3: Now, at that time Venerable Udāyī was sitting teaching Dhamma, surrounded by a large assembly of laypeople.
translation_text.an5.159:1.5: went up to the Buddha, bowed, sat down to one side, and said to him:
translation_text.an5.159:1.6: “Sir, Venerable Udāyī is teaching Dhamma, surrounded by a large assembly of laypeople.”
translation_text.an5.159:2.1: “Ānanda, it’s not easy to teach Dhamma to others.
translation_text.an5.159:2.2: You should establish five things in yourself before teaching Dhamma to others.
translation_text.an5.159:2.4: You should teach Dhamma to others thinking: ‘I will teach step by step.’ …
translation_text.an5.159:2.7: ‘I will not teach while secretly hoping to profit.’ …
translation_text.an5.159:2.8: ‘I will teach without damaging myself or others.’
translation_text.an5.159:2.9: It’s not easy to teach Dhamma to others.
translation_text.an5.159:2.10: You should establish these five things in yourself before teaching Dhamma to others.”

variant_text.an5.159:2.4: Anupubbiṁ kathaṁ → ānupubbikathaṁ (bj); anupubbikathaṁ (sya-all, pts1ed, mr)
